name: Meeting Seri (Blacksmith Variant)
callback_detail: She maps Thornwall in the ash by the forge vent. She adds you to the map.
text:
The new girl has a name nobody can pronounce right. She corrects them once, quietly, then stops trying. By the second week, everyone calls her Seri, which is close enough and not close at all.

She sits by the forge vent — the warm spot where the apprentices eat lunch — and draws in the ash with a stick. Not pictures. Maps. She's mapping Thornwall from memory, every building, every path, with an accuracy that makes the apprentices uncomfortable.

She looks up when you sit down. Studies your face the way she studies buildings — like she's adding you to the map.

'You're the smith's kid,' she says. It's not a question.

'You're the new girl,' you say. Also not a question.

She goes back to drawing. You eat your lunch. The silence between you is the comfortable kind, and you don't realize until years later that this was the most important meal of your life.
